_comment: Copy to config/models.json and configure your actual models.
_comment2: Never hardcode model names in application code — route by capability_class only.
_comment3: Multiple deployments with the same model_name = LiteLLM picks by latency/availability.
deployments: _tier0_comment: TIER 0 — FAST (general, non-code tasks: docs, generic)
_tier0_coder_comment: TIER 0 — CODER (code tasks: bug_fix, file_edit, test_write, refactor_small)

deployments._comment: DeepSeek R1 14B — fast reasoning, runs alongside coder model on 32GB VRAM

deployments: _tier2_comment: TIER 2 — HEAVY LOCAL (optional — requires 70B+ model, 40GB+ VRAM). Comment out if not available.

deployments._comment: Uncomment and set model when a 70B+ local model is available

deployments: _cloud_comment: CLOUD — PLANNER (architecture, replanning, meta-reasoning). All cloud usage is logged with model_source tag.)

deployments._comment: Primary cloud planner — Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6
DeepSeek Reasoner — chain-of-thought reasoning, cost-effective planner alternative
OpenAI GPT-4.1 — current OpenAI API flagship (as of Feb 2026)

deployments: _cloud_reasoning_comment: CLOUD — REASONING FALLBACKS (when local reasoning fails, before escalating to planner)

deployments._comment: DeepSeek Chat (V3.2) — strong general + code reasoning, cost-effective cloud fallback
OpenAI GPT-4.1 Mini — fast cloud reasoning fallback

deployments: _cloud_fast_comment: CLOUD — FAST FALLBACKS (when local fast model fails)

deployments._comment: Claude Haiku 4.5 — fastest Anthropic model, cheap cloud fallback for utility tasks
OpenAI GPT-4.1 Nano — smallest/cheapest OpenAI option for utility tasks
